---
title: "2_Measures"
author: "Arturo Bertero"
date: "2026-02-10"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
#clean
rm(list = ls())

library("pacman")
p_load(tidyverse, here, haven, countrycode, sjPlot, lme4, survey, psych,
       GPArotation, car, readxl, ggcorrplot) 

```


# Input

```{r}
# Export to RDS
lon_turn_df = readRDS(here("Input", "data_clean.rds"))
```

# Processing

## L

### Country means

```{r}
# 1. Clean and Recode 'lon_orig' to numeric
lon_turn_df <- lon_turn_df %>%
  mutate(lon_orig_n = case_when(
    lon_orig == "None or almost none of the time" ~ 1,
    lon_orig == "Some of the time"                ~ 2,
    lon_orig == "Most of the time"                ~ 3,
    lon_orig == "All or almost all of the time"   ~ 4,
    TRUE ~ NA_real_ # Handles "Don't know", "Refusal", "No answer"
  ))

# 2. Calculate Means and 95% Confidence Intervals by Country
lon_means_ci <- lon_turn_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(lon_orig_n)) %>%
  group_by(cntry) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_lon = mean(lon_orig_n, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_lon   = sd(lon_orig_n, na.rm = TRUE),
    n        = n()
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    se = sd_lon / sqrt(n),
    lower = mean_lon - 1.96 * se,
    upper = mean_lon + 1.96 * se
  ) %>%
  # Sort countries by decreasing mean loneliness
  arrange(desc(mean_lon)) %>%
  mutate(cntry = factor(cntry, levels = cntry))

# 3. Create the Visualization (Dot Plot with CIs)
p_lon_means <- ggplot(lon_means_ci, aes(x = cntry, y = mean_lon)) +
  geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), color = "#d95f02", size = 0.6) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(1, 2.5), breaks = seq(1, 4, by = 0.5)) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 9),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Average Loneliness by Country (4-Level Scale)",
    subtitle = "Mean scores with 95% Confidence Intervals. Ordered by decreasing loneliness.",
    x = "Country",
    y = "Mean Loneliness (1-4 Scale)",
    caption = "1: None | 2: Some | 3: Most | 4: All. Error bars represent 95% CI."
  )

# 4. Save the Plot
ggsave(here("Output", "Measures", "Lon_Means_CI_Original.png"), 
       p_lon_means, width = 11, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```


### Lon levels by country

```{r}
# Project: Lon_turn
# File 4: Descriptive - Loneliness Composition by Country

library(tidyverse)
library(here)

# 1. Prepare Data
# We filter out non-responses and define the order of the loneliness levels
lon_levels_ordered <- c(
  "None or almost none of the time",
  "Some of the time",
  "Most of the time",
  "All or almost all of the time"
)

audit_data_lon <- lon_turn_df %>%
  filter(lon_orig %in% lon_levels_ordered) %>%
  mutate(
    # Ensure factor order for the legend
    lon_orig = factor(lon_orig, levels = lon_levels_ordered),
    # Numeric version for ordering countries by mean
    lon_n = as.numeric(lon_orig)
  )

# 2. Calculate Country Order (by decreasing mean loneliness)
cntry_order_audit <- audit_data_lon %>%
  group_by(cntry) %>%
  summarise(m = mean(lon_n)) %>%
  arrange(desc(m)) %>%
  pull(cntry)

# 3. Calculate Proportions
plot_ready_lon <- audit_data_lon %>%
  group_by(cntry, lon_orig) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop_last") %>%
  mutate(prop = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(cntry = factor(cntry, levels = cntry_order_audit))

# 4. Create the Stacked Bar Plot (Inspired by EES Audit Code)
lon_composition_plot <- ggplot(plot_ready_lon, aes(x = cntry, y = prop, fill = lon_orig)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", width = 0.75, color = "white", linewidth = 0.1) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent, expand = c(0, 0)) +
  # Using viridis 'plasma' as requested for clear category separation
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "plasma", end = 0.8, direction = -1) +
  labs(
    title = "Loneliness Composition by Country",
    subtitle = "Proportion of population in each frequency category. Ordered by decreasing mean loneliness.",
    x = "Country",
    y = "Proportion of Sample",
    fill = "Frequency of Loneliness"
  ) +
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 9),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  ) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE))

# 5. Save as Landscape (Better for 30-country x-axis)
ggsave(here("Output", "Measures", "loneliness_proportions_by_country.png"), 
       plot = lon_composition_plot, 
       width = 12, 
       height = 7, 
       dpi = 600)
```


## SI

### Building the index

#### country-level corrs for dummy vars (SI)

```{r}
# Select SI dummies
# 1. Define final SI variables (no marit_iso)
si_vars <- c("hhsize_iso", "meet_iso", "relig_iso", 
             "volunt_iso", "discuss_iso", "unemp_iso")

# 2. Prepare the Correlation Data (Lower Triangle Only)
cor_long_full <- lon_turn_df %>%
  group_by(cntry) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(cor_data = map(data, function(df_sub) {
    # Calculate tetrachoric matrix with smoothing for zero cells
    r_mat <- suppressMessages(tetrachoric(df_sub[, si_vars], correct = 0.5)$rho)
    
    # Keep only lower triangle
    r_mat[upper.tri(r_mat, diag = TRUE)] <- NA
    
    # Convert to long format
    as.data.frame(as.table(r_mat)) %>%
      rename(Var1 = Var1, Var2 = Var2, Cor = Freq) %>%
      filter(!is.na(Cor))
  })) %>%
  select(cntry, cor_data) %>%
  unnest(cor_data)

# 3. Split Countries into Two Batches
unique_countries <- unique(cor_long_full$cntry)
batch1 <- unique_countries[1:15]
batch2 <- unique_countries[16:length(unique_countries)]

# 4. Plot Function with geom_text for cell labels
plot_si_cors_labeled <- function(data_subset, batch_name) {
  ggplot(data_subset, aes(Var1, Var2, fill = Cor)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white") +
    # Add correlation labels rounded to 2 digits
    geom_text(aes(label = round(Cor, 2)), size = 2.5, color = "black") +
    scale_fill_gradient2(low = "red", high = "blue", mid = "white", 
                         midpoint = 0, limit = c(-1, 1), 
                         name = "Correlation") +
    facet_wrap(~cntry, ncol = 3) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1, size = 8),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = NA),
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "right"
    ) +
    labs(title = paste("Social Isolation Correlation Structure -", batch_name),
         subtitle = "Tetrachoric Correlations (Lower Triangle). Values show degree of association between isolated poles.",
         x = NULL, y = NULL)
}

# 5. Generate and Save
p_batch1_lab <- plot_si_cors_labeled(filter(cor_long_full, cntry %in% batch1), "Batch 1")
p_batch2_lab <- plot_si_cors_labeled(filter(cor_long_full, cntry %in% batch2), "Batch 2")

# Save plots - ensuring directory exists
ggsave(here("Output", "Measures", "SI_Correlations_Labels_B1.png"), p_batch1_lab, width = 12, height = 15)
ggsave(here("Output", "Measures", "SI_Correlations_Labels_B2.png"), p_batch2_lab, width = 12, height = 15)
```

#### Country-level levels of SI vars

```{r}
# 1. Prepare the prevalence data
# We calculate the % of '1' (Isolated) for each variable per country
si_dist_data <- lon_turn_df %>%
  select(cntry, hhsize_iso, meet_iso, relig_iso, 
         volunt_iso, discuss_iso, unemp_iso) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -cntry, names_to = "indicator", values_to = "is_isolated") %>%
  group_by(cntry, indicator) %>%
  summarise(
    prevalence = mean(is_isolated, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, # Convert to percentage
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  # Clean up labels for the plot
  mutate(indicator = str_remove(indicator, "_iso"))

# 2. Split Countries into Two Batches for readability
unique_countries <- unique(si_dist_data$cntry)
batch1 <- unique_countries[1:15]
batch2 <- unique_countries[16:length(unique_countries)]

# 3. Plot Function
plot_si_distributions <- function(data_subset, batch_name) {
  ggplot(data_subset, aes(x = indicator, y = prevalence, fill = indicator)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", show.legend = FALSE) +
    geom_text(aes(label = round(prevalence, 1)), vjust = -0.5, size = 2.5) +
    facet_wrap(~cntry, ncol = 3) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 25)) +
    scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set3") +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = NA),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Prevalence of Social Isolation Markers -", batch_name),
      subtitle = "Percentage (%) of respondents in the 'Isolated' pole for each indicator",
      x = "Isolation Indicator",
      y = "% Isolated"
    )
}

# 4. Generate and Save
p_dist_b1 <- plot_si_distributions(filter(si_dist_data, cntry %in% batch1), "Batch 1")
p_dist_b2 <- plot_si_distributions(filter(si_dist_data, cntry %in% batch2), "Batch 2")

# Ensure directory exists
ggsave(here("Output", "Measures", "SI_Distributions_B1.png"), p_dist_b1, width = 12, height = 15)
ggsave(here("Output", "Measures", "SI_Distributions_B2.png"), p_dist_b2, width = 12, height = 15)
```


#### VIF

```{r}
# 2. Run the VIF loop by country
vif_by_country <- lon_turn_df %>%
  group_by(cntry) %>%
  group_split() %>%
  map_dfr(function(df_country) {
    current_country <- unique(df_country$cntry)
    
    # Check for zero variance in indicators within the country 
    # (VIF can't be calculated if a variable is 100% 0 or 1 in that country)
    var_check <- df_country %>%
      summarise(across(all_of(si_vars), ~var(.x, na.rm = TRUE))) %>%
      pivot_longer(everything()) %>%
      filter(value > 0)
    
    valid_vars <- var_check$name
    
    # If we have at least 2 variables with variance, run the model
    if(length(valid_vars) >= 2) {
      form <- as.formula(paste("as.numeric(vote) ~", paste(valid_vars, collapse = " + ")))
      model <- lm(form, data = df_country)
      
      # Extract VIF and convert to dataframe
      vif_values <- vif(model)
      
      data.frame(
        cntry = current_country,
        indicator = names(vif_values),
        vif = as.numeric(vif_values)
      )
    } else {
      data.frame(cntry = current_country, indicator = "Insufficient Variance", vif = NA)
    }
  })

# 3. Pivot for a clean table format
vif_table_final <- vif_by_country %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = indicator, values_from = vif) %>%
  arrange(cntry)

#visualize
vif_table_final

# 4. save 
write_csv(vif_table_final, here("Output", "Measures", "SI_VIF_by_Country.csv"))

# Inspect the "worst" cases (highest VIFs)
vif_by_country %>% arrange(desc(vif)) %>% head(10)
```

#### Validation

```{r}
# 1. Prepare Data: Update to si_index_raw
validity_long <- lon_turn_df %>%
  mutate(
    lon_n      = as.numeric(lon),
    gender_n   = as.numeric(gender),
    educ_num   = as.numeric(educ),
    inc_num    = 5 - as.numeric(hhincome),
    # Use the raw additive index (0-7) for Spearman correlation
    si_raw_n   = as.numeric(si_index_raw) 
  ) %>%
  select(cntry, si_raw_n, lon_n, gender_n, age, educ_num, inc_num) %>%
  group_by(cntry) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(cor_data = map(data, function(df_sub) {
    # Spearman is robust for the ordinal/count nature of these vars
    r_mat <- cor(df_sub, method = "spearman", use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
    
    # Filter for the SI Index row only
    as.data.frame(as.table(r_mat)) %>%
      filter(Var1 == "si_raw_n" & Var2 != "si_raw_n") %>%
      rename(Variable = Var2, Cor = Freq) %>%
      mutate(Variable = case_when(
        Variable == "lon_n"    ~ "Loneliness",
        Variable == "gender_n" ~ "Gender",
        Variable == "age"      ~ "Age",
        Variable == "educ_num" ~ "Education",
        Variable == "inc_num"  ~ "Income"
      ))
  })) %>%
  select(cntry, cor_data) %>%
  unnest(cor_data)

# 2. Plotting Function (Same logic, cleaner theme)
plot_si_profile_updated <- function(data_subset, batch_name) {
  ggplot(data_subset, aes(x = Variable, y = Cor, fill = Cor)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", color = "white") +
    geom_text(aes(label = round(Cor, 2)), 
              # Dynamic positioning based on bar direction
              hjust = ifelse(data_subset$Cor >= 0, -0.2, 1.2), size = 2.5) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(low = "red", high = "blue", mid = "white", 
                         midpoint = 0, limit = c(-1, 1), name = "Spearman r") +
    facet_wrap(~cntry, ncol = 3) +
    coord_flip() + 
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-0.6, 0.6)) +
    theme(
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = NA),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    ) +
    labs(title = paste("SI Index Validity Profile -", batch_name),
         subtitle = "Correlations based on the raw 0-7 SI Index",
         x = NULL, y = "Spearman Correlation")
}

# 3. Save Batches
unique_countries <- unique(validity_long$cntry)
p_profile_b1 <- plot_si_profile_updated(filter(validity_long, cntry %in% unique_countries[1:15]), "Batch 1")
p_profile_b2 <- plot_si_profile_updated(filter(validity_long, cntry %in% unique_countries[16:length(unique_countries)]), "Batch 2")

ggsave(here("Output", "Measures", "SI_Validity_Profile_B1.png"), p_profile_b1, width = 12, height = 14)
ggsave(here("Output", "Measures", "SI_Validity_Profile_B2.png"), p_profile_b2, width = 12, height = 14)
```

### Descriptives on the final index

#### SI country - Means

```{r}
# Calculate Means and CIs for si_n (0-6 scale)
si_means_ci <- lon_turn_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(si_n)) %>%
  group_by(cntry) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_si = mean(si_n, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_si   = sd(si_n, na.rm = TRUE),
    n       = n()
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    se = sd_si / sqrt(n),
    lower = mean_si - 1.96 * se,
    upper = mean_si + 1.96 * se
  ) %>%
  # Order countries by decreasing Social Isolation
  arrange(desc(mean_si)) %>%
  mutate(cntry = factor(cntry, levels = cntry))

# Create Dot-Range Plot
p_si_means <- ggplot(si_means_ci, aes(x = cntry, y = mean_si)) +
  geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), color = "#7570b3", size = 0.6) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 9),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Average Social Isolation by Country",
    subtitle = "Mean scores on the 0-6 index with 95% CIs. Ordered by decreasing isolation.",
    x = "Country",
    y = "Mean SI Index (0-6)",
    caption = "Index counts markers like living alone, infrequent social meetings, etc. Higher = More Isolated."
  )

# --- 2. Plot B: Stacked Proportions (Composition Audit) ---

# Define the levels for SI
si_levels_order <- c("0-1: Low ISO", "2: Mid ISO", "3+: High ISO")

plot_ready_si <- lon_turn_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(si_levels)) %>%
  mutate(si_levels = factor(si_levels, levels = si_levels_order)) %>%
  group_by(cntry, si_levels) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop_last") %>%
  mutate(prop = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  # Reuse the country order from the means plot for alignment
  mutate(cntry = factor(cntry, levels = levels(si_means_ci$cntry)))

# Create the Stacked Bar Plot
p_si_proportions <- ggplot(plot_ready_si, aes(x = cntry, y = prop, fill = si_levels)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", width = 0.75, color = "white", linewidth = 0.1) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent, expand = c(0, 0)) +
  # Viridis 'plasma' for consistency with the loneliness audit
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "plasma", end = 0.8) +
  labs(
    title = "Social Isolation Composition by Country",
    subtitle = "Proportion of population by isolation intensity. Ordered by decreasing mean isolation.",
    x = "Country",
    y = "Proportion of Sample",
    fill = "Isolation Level"
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 9),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  )

# --- 3. Save Both ---
ggsave(here("Output", "Measures", "SI_Means_CI.png"), p_si_means, width = 11, height = 6, dpi = 600)
ggsave(here("Output", "Measures", "SI_Proportions_Stacked.png"), p_si_proportions, width = 12, height = 7, dpi = 600)
```


## SI-L

### Typology plot

```{r}
# 1. Prepare frequency and percentage data
sil_dist <- lon_turn_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(sil_type)) %>%
  group_by(cntry, sil_type) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop_last") %>%
  mutate(pct = (n / sum(n)) * 100) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    Lon_Pos = if_else(str_detect(sil_type, "High L"), "High Loneliness", "Low Loneliness"),
    SI_Pos  = if_else(str_detect(sil_type, "High SI"), "High SI", "Low SI"),
    Lon_Pos = factor(Lon_Pos, levels = c("Low Loneliness", "High Loneliness")),
    SI_Pos  = factor(SI_Pos, levels = c("Low SI", "High SI"))
  )

# 2. Split Countries into Two Batches
unique_countries <- unique(sil_dist$cntry)
batch1 <- unique_countries[1:15]
batch2 <- unique_countries[16:length(unique_countries)]

# 3. Plotting Function for the 2x2 Heatmap (Single Color Shade)
plot_sil_heatmap_single <- function(data_subset, batch_name) {
  ggplot(data_subset, aes(x = Lon_Pos, y = SI_Pos, fill = pct)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white", size = 0.5) +
    # Cell labels
    geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(pct, 1), "%")), 
              color = "black", fontface = "bold", size = 5) +
    # SINGLE COLOR SCALE: Shades of Blue
    # Using light blue for low % and deep navy for high %
    scale_fill_gradient(low = "#f7fbff", high = "blue", name = "% of Pop") +
    facet_wrap(~cntry, ncol = 3) +
    theme(
      axis.title = element_blank(),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 7),
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = NA),
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Distribution of SIL Typology -", batch_name),
      subtitle = "2x2 Landscape of Isolation & Loneliness (Relative thresholds per country)",
      caption = "Bottom-Left: Integrated | Top-Right: Double Burden (High L + High SI)"
    )
}

# 4. Generate and Save
p_heat_b1_v2 <- plot_sil_heatmap_single(filter(sil_dist, cntry %in% batch1), "Batch 1")
p_heat_b2_v2 <- plot_sil_heatmap_single(filter(sil_dist, cntry %in% batch2), "Batch 2")

ggsave(here("Output", "Measures", "SIL_Heatmap_SingleColor_B1.png"), p_heat_b1_v2, width = 12, height = 15)
ggsave(here("Output", "Measures", "SIL_Heatmap_SingleColor_B2.png"), p_heat_b2_v2, width = 12, height = 15)
```
### Audit plot

```{r}
# 1. Prepare Data
# Ensure sil_type is clean and ordered correctly for a logical stack
# Stack order: Integrated (bottom) -> High SI -> High L -> Double Burden (top)
sil_levels_ordered <- c("Low L, Low SI", "Low L, High SI", 
                        "High L, Low SI", "High L, High SI")

audit_data_sil <- lon_turn_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(sil_type)) %>%
  mutate(
    sil_type = factor(sil_type, levels = sil_levels_ordered),
    # Numeric version to help with country ordering (High L + High SI weights)
    sil_n = as.numeric(sil_type)
  )

# 2. Calculate Country Order
# We'll order by the proportion of the 'Double Burden' group (High L, High SI)
# This makes it easy to see where the problem is most acute
cntry_order_sil <- audit_data_sil %>%
  group_by(cntry) %>%
  summarise(double_burden_prop = mean(sil_type == "High L, High SI")) %>%
  arrange(desc(double_burden_prop)) %>%
  pull(cntry)

# 3. Calculate Proportions
plot_ready_sil <- audit_data_sil %>%
  group_by(cntry, sil_type) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop_last") %>%
  mutate(prop = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(cntry = factor(cntry, levels = cntry_order_sil))

# 4. Create the Stacked Bar Plot
sil_composition_plot <- ggplot(plot_ready_sil, aes(x = cntry, y = prop, fill = sil_type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", width = 0.75, color = "white", linewidth = 0.1) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent, expand = c(0, 0)) +
  # Using viridis 'plasma' to highlight the 'High' groups with brighter colors
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "plasma", end = 0.9, direction = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "SIL Typology Composition by Country",
    subtitle = "Proportion of population by Loneliness (L) and Social Isolation (SI) groups. Ordered by 'Double Burden' share.",
    x = "Country",
    y = "Proportion of Sample",
    fill = "Typology Group",
    caption = "Source: ESS Round 11. Groups defined by country-specific median thresholds."
  ) +
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 9),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  ) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE))

# 5. Save as Landscape
ggsave(here("Output", "Measures", "sil_typology_proportions_by_country.png"), 
       plot = sil_composition_plot, 
       width = 12, 
       height = 7, 
       dpi = 600)
```


## SI with turnout

```{r}
# 1. Calculate Predicted Probabilities and 95% CIs by Country
# We use si_levels (the 0-1, 2, 3+ factor)
si_levels_turnout_ci <- lon_turn_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(si_levels) & !is.na(vote)) %>%
  group_by(cntry) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(preds = map(data, function(df_sub) {
    # Fit logistic model with the new 3-level factor
    m_desc <- glm(vote ~ si_levels, data = df_sub, family = binomial)
    
    # Create prediction data for the 3 levels
    pred_df <- data.frame(si_levels = levels(df_sub$si_levels))
    
    # Get predictions on the logit scale
    preds_link <- predict(m_desc, newdata = pred_df, type = "link", se.fit = TRUE)
    
    # Back-transform to probability scale
    pred_df %>%
      mutate(
        prob = plogis(preds_link$fit),
        lower = plogis(preds_link$fit - 1.96 * preds_link$se.fit),
        upper = plogis(preds_link$fit + 1.96 * preds_link$se.fit)
      )
  })) %>%
  select(cntry, preds) %>%
  unnest(preds)

# 2. Split Countries into Two Batches
unique_countries <- unique(si_levels_turnout_ci$cntry)
batch1 <- unique_countries[1:15]
batch2 <- unique_countries[16:length(unique_countries)]

# 3. Plotting Function: Using the new factor levels
plot_si_levels_turnout <- function(data_subset, batch_name) {
  ggplot(data_subset, aes(x = si_levels, y = prob, color = si_levels)) +
    geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), size = 0.7, fatten = 2) +
    facet_wrap(~cntry, ncol = 3) +
    # Consistent 50-100% trim for European turnout
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(), limits = c(0.4, 1.0)) +
    # Dark2 palette provides good contrast for the 3 distinct groups
    scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_blank(), # Labels are in the legend
      axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = NA),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Turnout by Social Isolation Intensity -", batch_name),
      subtitle = "Predicted Probability of Voting (Y-axis trimmed to 50-100%)",
      x = "SI Level (Low → Mid → High)",
      y = "Prob. of Voting",
      color = "SI Intensity"
    )
}

# 4. Generate and Save
p_si_levels_b1 <- plot_si_levels_turnout(filter(si_levels_turnout_ci, cntry %in% batch1), "Batch 1")
p_si_levels_b2 <- plot_si_levels_turnout(filter(si_levels_turnout_ci, cntry %in% batch2), "Batch 2")

ggsave(here("Output", "Measures", "SI_Levels_Turnout_B1.png"), p_si_levels_b1, width = 12, height = 15)
ggsave(here("Output", "Measures", "SI_Levels_Turnout_B2.png"), p_si_levels_b2, width = 12, height = 15)
```


## lon with turnout

```{r}
# 1. Define the updated levels from File 1
lon_3_ordered <- c("None/Almost none", "Some of the time", "Most/All the time")

# 2. Calculate Predicted Probabilities and 95% CIs
# Using lon_3levels created in File 1
lon_3level_turnout_ci <- lon_turn_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(lon) & !is.na(vote)) %>%
  group_by(cntry) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(preds = map(data, function(df_sub) {
    # Fit logistic model with the 3-level factor
    m_desc <- glm(vote ~ lon, data = df_sub, family = binomial)
    
    # Prediction data for the 3 levels
    pred_df <- data.frame(
      lon = factor(lon_3_ordered, levels = lon_3_ordered)
    )
    
    # Get predictions
    preds_link <- predict(m_desc, newdata = pred_df, type = "link", se.fit = TRUE)
    
    pred_df %>%
      mutate(
        prob = plogis(preds_link$fit),
        lower = plogis(preds_link$fit - 1.96 * preds_link$se.fit),
        upper = plogis(preds_link$fit + 1.96 * preds_link$se.fit)
      )
  })) %>%
  select(cntry, preds) %>%
  unnest(preds)

# 3. Simplified Plotting Function
plot_lon_3level_ordered <- function(data_subset, batch_name) {
  ggplot(data_subset, aes(x = lon, y = prob, color = lon)) +
    geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), size = 0.7, fatten = 2.5) +
    facet_wrap(~cntry, ncol = 3) +
    # Using your 30%-100% trim to show the full range of the 'drop'
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(), limits = c(0.3, 1.0)) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_blank(), 
      axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = NA),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.text = element_text(size = 8)
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Turnout by Loneliness Intensity -", batch_name),
      subtitle = "Ordered dosage effect: None → Some → Most/All",
      x = NULL, y = "Prob. of Voting",
      color = "Loneliness Level"
    )
}

# 4. Generate and Save
unique_countries <- unique(lon_3level_turnout_ci$cntry)
p_lon_final_b1 <- plot_lon_3level_ordered(filter(lon_3level_turnout_ci, cntry %in% unique_countries[1:15]), "Batch 1")
p_lon_final_b2 <- plot_lon_3level_ordered(filter(lon_3level_turnout_ci, cntry %in% unique_countries[16:length(unique_countries)]), "Batch 2")

ggsave(here("Output", "Measures", "Lon_Turnout1.png"), p_lon_final_b1, width = 12, height = 15)
ggsave(here("Output", "Measures", "Lon_Turnout2.png"), p_lon_final_b2, width = 12, height = 15)
```


## SI-L with turnout

```{r}
# 1. Calculate Predicted Probabilities and 95% CIs by Country
# We use a logistic regression for each country to get the standard errors
sil_turnout_ci <- lon_turn_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(sil_type) & !is.na(vote)) %>%
  group_by(cntry) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(preds = map(data, function(df_sub) {
    # Fit simple logistic model
    m_desc <- glm(vote ~ sil_type, data = df_sub, family = binomial)
    
    # Create prediction data for the 4 levels
    pred_df <- data.frame(sil_type = levels(df_sub$sil_type))
    
    # Get predictions on the link scale (logit) to calculate CIs
    preds_link <- predict(m_desc, newdata = pred_df, type = "link", se.fit = TRUE)
    
    # Back-transform to probability scale with 95% CI
    pred_df %>%
      mutate(
        prob = plogis(preds_link$fit),
        lower = plogis(preds_link$fit - 1.96 * preds_link$se.fit),
        upper = plogis(preds_link$fit + 1.96 * preds_link$se.fit)
      )
  })) %>%
  select(cntry, preds) %>%
  unnest(preds)

# 2. Define Batches for the 30 countries
unique_countries <- unique(sil_turnout_ci$cntry)
batch1 <- unique_countries[1:15]
batch2 <- unique_countries[16:length(unique_countries)]

# 3. Plotting Function: Dot Plot with geom_pointrange
plot_sil_turnout_ci <- function(data_subset, batch_name) {
  ggplot(data_subset, aes(x = sil_type, y = prob, color = sil_type)) +
    # Use geom_pointrange to show the mean and the CI bar
    geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), size = 0.5) +
    facet_wrap(~cntry, ncol = 3) +
    # Trim Y axis as requested (50% to 100%)
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(), limits = c(0.3, 1)) +
    scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2", direction = -1) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_blank(), # Profile names are in the legend
      axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = NA),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Turnout by SIL Typology (with 95% CI) -", batch_name),
      subtitle = "Predicted Probability of Voting (Y-axis trimmed to 50-100%)",
      x = "Profile (Integrated → Double Burden)",
      y = "Prob. of Voting",
      color = "Profile"
    )
}

# 4. Generate and Save
p_turn_ci_b1 <- plot_sil_turnout_ci(filter(sil_turnout_ci, cntry %in% batch1), "Batch 1")
p_turn_ci_b2 <- plot_sil_turnout_ci(filter(sil_turnout_ci, cntry %in% batch2), "Batch 2")

# Ensure directory exists
dir.create(here("Output", "Measures"), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(here("Output", "Measures", "SIL_Turnout_CI_B1.png"), p_turn_ci_b1, width = 12, height = 15)
ggsave(here("Output", "Measures", "SIL_Turnout_CI_B2.png"), p_turn_ci_b2, width = 12, height = 15)
```

## Vote, vote_strict, official

```{r}
# --- 1. Import and Clean IDEA Data ---
# We ensure strictly one official measure per ISO2 code
idea_clean <- read_excel(here("Input", "idea.xlsx")) %>%
  select(ISO2, year, Voter_Turnout = `Voter Turnout`) %>%
  # Convert '45.23%' to 0.4523
  mutate(
    official = as.numeric(gsub("%", "", Voter_Turnout)) / 100,
    election_year = as.numeric(substr(year, 1, 4))
  ) %>%
  # PROBLEM FIX: Keep only the most recent election year per country
  group_by(ISO2) %>%
  slice_max(election_year, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(ISO2, official)

# --- 2. Aggregate Survey Data (ESS) ---
survey_agg <- lon_turn_df %>%
  group_by(cntry) %>%
  summarise(
    vote        = mean(vote, na.rm = TRUE),
    vote_strict = mean(vote_strict, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# --- 3. Join and Prepare for Plotting ---
validation_long <- survey_agg %>%
  inner_join(idea_clean, by = c("cntry" = "ISO2")) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(official, vote, vote_strict),
    names_to = "measure",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  # PROBLEM FIX: Set the specific order for the bars
  mutate(measure = factor(measure, levels = c("official", "vote", "vote_strict")))

# --- 4. Generate Faceted Bar Chart ---
# We split into two batches for readability (15 countries each)
unique_cntries <- unique(validation_long$cntry)
batch1 <- unique_cntries[1:15]
batch2 <- unique_cntries[16:length(unique_cntries)]

plot_validation_bars <- function(data_subset, batch_label) {
  ggplot(data_subset, aes(x = measure, y = value, fill = measure)) +
    geom_col(color = "white", width = 0.7) +
    # Add percentage labels on top of bars
    geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(value * 100, 0), "%")), 
              vjust = -0.5, size = 2.5) +
    facet_wrap(~ cntry, ncol = 3) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(), limits = c(0, 1.1)) +
    # Consistent color scheme
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("official" = "#333333", "vote" = "#F8766D", "vote_strict" = "#00BFC4")) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_blank(), # Legend handles the labels
      axis.title.x = element_blank(),
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = NA),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Turnout Validation:", batch_label),
      subtitle = "Comparing Official Turnout vs. Self-Reported vs. Strict Vote Recall",
      y = "Turnout Rate (%)",
      fill = "Measure"
    )
}

# 5. Save Batches
p_v_b1 <- plot_validation_bars(filter(validation_long, cntry %in% batch1), "Batch 1")
p_v_b2 <- plot_validation_bars(filter(validation_long, cntry %in% batch2), "Batch 2")

ggsave(here("Output", "Measures", "Turnout_Validation_Bars_B1.png"), p_v_b1, width = 12, height = 15)
ggsave(here("Output", "Measures", "Turnout_Validation_Bars_B2.png"), p_v_b2, width = 12, height = 15)
```
